text.title: Recognition Over Recall Examples and Audit Patterns
text.summary: Real-world good and bad examples, common mistakes, and agentic audit workflow with gotchas.

example.note: Google Search: recent searches shown on focus, autocomplete suggestions, "Did you mean" corrections, related searches. All are recognition aids that eliminate recall of prior queries.
VS Code Command Palette (Ctrl+P): fuzzy search for commands, recent files shown, icons and labels in menus. Turns command recall into command recognition.
Spotify: album art thumbnails, recently played prominent on home screen, visual playlists. Users recognize what they want to play; they do not recall album names.
"Enter product code: ___" — user must recall an arbitrary alphanumeric code. Better: product picker with images and names. Recognition replaces recall entirely.
Multi-step checkout where prior address entered on step 1 is not visible on step 3. User must recall what they typed. Better: summary sidebar showing all prior selections.
Toolbar with 12 icons and no labels. Users must memorize what each icon means. Better: icon + text label for all actions, tooltip fallback for space-constrained areas.

antipattern.description: Icon-only toolbars where users must memorize icon meanings.
antipattern.reason: Icons without labels require recall of meaning. Even "universal" icons (save floppy disk, hamburger menu) are not universally recognized by all user populations.

antipattern.description: Multi-step forms with no progress summary of prior selections.
antipattern.reason: Cross-screen recall burden is the most common form of heuristic #6 violation and is invisible in static design reviews — requires prototype testing to detect.

section.title: Agentic Audit Workflow
section.p: Agent receives UI screen descriptions, wireframe annotations, or component specs. Applies recognition audit template per screen: identifies recall demands, hidden options, icon-only elements, missing autocomplete, and cross-screen context failures. Outputs structured findings with severity and recognition alternative per item.
section.reference: Recognition audit template for systematic per-screen review
LLM prompt for auditing a screen against this heuristic

antipattern.description: Agent flags all icon-only elements including universally understood icons (magnifying glass for search, X for close).
antipattern.reason: Provide agent a list of safe icon exceptions before running audit. Magnifying glass and X are recognized across all user populations and do not need labels.

antipattern.description: Agent audits static wireframes and misses cross-screen recall failures.
antipattern.reason: Information visible on screen A but gone on screen B is a dynamic property. Note this limitation explicitly in audit output; require prototype testing to validate cross-screen context.
